svg.text: DAQIRI RX Path — Pointer Lifecycle & Multiple Receive Modes
DPDK backend · ConnectX-6/7 · GPUDirect via peermem or DMA-BUF (or host_pinned on integrated GPUs)

g.text: Matches on UDP src/dst, IPv4 length, or flex-item bytes after UDP header. Action = queue.id.
rte_mbuf * — one descriptor pair per packet (seg-0 + seg-1 mbuf chain)
Filled by NIC DMA · drained by rte_eth_rx_burst() in worker
Worker enqueues · user dequeues via get_rx_burst()
1 memory_region → segment 0 (full packet in GPU VRAM)
DMA-mapped via peermem/DMA-BUF; full packet lands
in VRAM in one shot — no segmentation.
rte_mbuf * — one descriptor per packet (single segment)
Filled by NIC DMA · drained by rte_eth_rx_burst()
Worker enqueues · user dequeues via get_rx_burst()
Owns the BurstParams* between get_rx_burst() and the matching free. Reads from packet buffers in place — zero-copy.
hdr = get_segment_packet_ptr(burst, 0, i); pay = get_segment_packet_ptr(burst, 1, i);
ptr = get_packet_ptr(burst, i); flow_id = get_packet_flow_id(burst, i);
Buffer return path — packets first, then burst (MUST NOT LEAK)
free_rx_burst() releases only the BurstParams handle. It does NOT free packet buffers — those must be returned to the mempool first.
Skipping the per-packet step drains the packet mempool → NO_FREE_PACKET_BUFFERS → NIC drops new traffic.
Frees every packet (all segments) + the burst
// works for HDS & batched, any segment count
Free each segment's packet pool, then release
Use when only some packets are kept (e.g. forwarded
to another stage). Then close the burst.
• free_packet* / free_all_segment_packets → returns rte_mbuf to the segment's packet mempool (CPU hugepage / GPU device).
• free_rx_burst → returns the BurstParams handle to the rx_meta_buffers pool (separate from the packet pool).
• free_all_packets_and_burst_rx = both of the above, in order.
Rule of thumb: num_bufs ≥ 3× batch_size; DPDK auto-bumps to 3× ring (default 8192 → 24576) and logs WARN if too small (deadlock guard).
Symptoms of a missed free: NO_FREE_PACKET_BUFFERS / NO_FREE_BURST_BUFFERS, then NIC-level RX drops in imissed.
① Number of memory_regions per queue picks the receive mode (1 = batched / CPU-only, 2 = header-data split). See docs/configuration.md §Memory Regions.
② Same flow rule schema serves both columns: rx.flows[*].action.id chooses the queue; mode is determined by that queue's memory_regions list.
③ On integrated GPUs (e.g. NVIDIA GB10 / DGX Spark) substitute kind: host_pinned for device — the NIC can't peer-DMA into iGPU VRAM.
④ Sources: include/daqiri/common.h (API), include/daqiri/types.h (MemoryKind), src/managers/dpdk/daqiri_dpdk_mgr.cpp (rte_flow), docs/configuration.md.
